rationale: The citing paper uses Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) as a key anchor for the hedonic-versus-utilitarian choice framework, explicitly invoking that literature when motivating its core prediction. The entire set of hypotheses and experiments is organized around this same choice contrast, so the citation helps structure the conceptual setup rather than serving as a passing background list item. Although the 2012 paper proposes a different mechanism (attribute numerosity and perceived usefulness), it relies on the cited paper as prior foundational work on this decision domain.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on the hedonic-versus-utilitarian choice framework established by the cited paper to examine a new driver of preference, attribute quantity. It uses the cited work as conceptual grounding for the choice context while introducing a different explanatory process.
